observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
• require the City General Services Officer (CGSO) to request from the Accounting Office the details and descriptions of the adjustments P23,595,100.07; and
• require the City Accountant to confer with the CGSO and to verify the quantity and amounts of the PPE recorded in lump sum amounts totaling P5,373,841.56. Also, we reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor require the CGSO and the City Accountant to reconcile their records, submit to the Audit Team the proof of reconciliation and any adjustments, if warranted, should be made in order that the existence and valuation of the PPE could be ascertained/presented for the fair presentation of the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: No action had been undertaken by Management during CY 2021.
observations.reason: No adjustments had been made in the RPCPPE and the lump-sum amounts recorded with no details and descriptions increased to P19,237,820.78. The RPCPPE and the PPE schedules were not yet reconciled. The observation was first raised in CY 2017 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the City Mayor require the:

a. City General Services Officer (CGSO) to:
• complete the conduct of physical count of all supplies and materials inventory of the City every semester and prepare and render a complete Report of the Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI) and submit to the COA Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for first and second semesters, respectively; and
• prepare monthly Report of Issues and Supplies/Materials (SSMI) based on the Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) and submit to the City Accounting Office in support to the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) recognizing the issued materials as expenses; and

b. City Accountant to:
• submit to the Audit Team the SSMI for issued materials for verification; and
• maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) for each commodity/stock to facilitate the reconciliation and for verification of the year-end balances; and

c. CGSO and the City Accountant to coordinate for the periodic reconciliation of their records and make the necessary adjustments in their respective records, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: No action had been undertaken by Management during CY 2021.
observations.reason: No actual physical count of inventories was conducted during the year. No RPCI was submitted to COA Audit Team on July 31, 2021 and January 31, 2022. The Accounting Office continued maintaining SLCs per account title instead of per commodity/stock. The RPCI was not submitted and SLCs per commodity/stock were not available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that the prospective contractor/winning bidder receive the Notice to Proceed within seven days upon approval/issuance of the contract in accordance with Section 37.4.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to avoid the delays in the implementation of the projects of the City.
observations.managementAction: No action had been undertaken by Management during CY 2021.
observations.reason: NTPs for 12 infrastructure projects in CY 2021 were still received late by the contractors/winning bidders. The observation was first raised in CY 2020 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Management require the CGSO and the City Accountant to provide the details of the PPE recorded in lump sum amounts totaling P23.595 million and P19.238 million, respectively; reconcile their records and submit to the Audit Team the proof of reconciliation and any adjustments, if warranted.

Henceforth, ensure that ledger cards/schedules maintained by the City Accountant and property cards by the CGSO contain the details of the PPE in compliance with Section 114 of MNGAs for LGUs, Volume I.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Management require the:

a. CGSO to:
• complete the conduct of physical count of all supplies and materials
inventory of the City every semester; prepare and render a complete Report
on Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI) and submit to the Audit Team not
later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for first and second
semesters, respectively;
• prepare and submit monthly SSMI based on the RIS to the City Accounting
Office in support to the JEV recognizing the issued materials as expenses.
Also, the City Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the SSMI for issued
materials for verification;

b. City Accountant to:
• submit to the Audit Team the SSMI for issued materials for verification;
• maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) for each commodity/stock to
facilitate the reconciliation and for verification of the year-end balances; and

c. CGSO and the City Accountant to coordinate for the periodic reconciliation of
their records and make the necessary adjustments in their respective records,
if warranted.

We recommended that the Management instruct the:
• City Accountant to seek or locate the Memorandum of Agreement entered into
with the Source Agencies or any documents stating the purpose of the fund
transfer and the provision of the requirements in implementing and reporting of
the unutilized PPAs totaling P12.608 million;
• City Planning and Development Office to facilitate the implementation of the
specific projects with funds received from NGAs totaling P11.822 million since the
resources are readily available, and in order not to deprive the City of the
projects/programs/activities where the funds are intended for;
• City Treasurer to remit the unexpended fund balance of P0.786 million to the
source agencies so that it could be used in other projects or by other agencies
needing assistance; and
• City Accountant to immediately liquidate the outstanding fund transfers from
national government agencies upon completion of the project or fulfillment of the
purpose for which the fund is intended for, in accordance with Section 4 of COA
Circular No. 94-013.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management require the Bids and
Awards Committee to ensure that the prospective contractor/winning bidder
receive the Notice to Proceed within seven days upon approval/issuance of the
contract in accordance with Section 37.4.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184
to avoid delays in the implementation of the projects of the City.

Also, submit to the Audit Team copies of NTPs for all the contracts executed during
CY 2021 for further review and evaluation.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
• continue and finish the conduct of survey of those hereditary properties which has been started by the previous administration and to seek the report of the Land Management Services of the DENR for the titling of the land;
• coordinate with the heirs of the deceased owner their necessary documents initially requested for three parcels of land; and
• follow-up the approval of the Land Management Services of the DENR for the titling of the land to prove ownership and to avoid possible third-party claims and incurrence of litigation costs in the future in pursuant to Section 39(2) of PD No.1445 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and for the fair presentation of the account in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Management already exerted their effort to follow-up the necessary documents for the parcels of land. The Management already identified the properties with inheritance issues and found them in the inventory report of Land of the City.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
• City Accountant to: coordinate with the City Engineering Office regarding the projects undertaken/completed on a phase-by-phase basis and make the necessary adjustments to reclassify the 21 completed infrastructure projects worth P19,474,192.95 to their respective PPE accounts; and request from the City Engineering Office the status of the 13 infrastructure projects undertaken in CYs 2017 to 2020 with a total cost of P6,812,109.21 and one project for upgrading of electrical facility of health center undertaken in CY 2010 with contract amount of P109,191.65 which are still recorded in the CIP account and prepare the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, if warranted.
• ICO-City Engineering Office to: review and inspect the infrastructure projects reviewed and implemented by their Office and submit to the Audit Team and the Accounting Office the Updated Status of Implementation of Infrastructure Projects undertaken in CYs 2017-2020. Thereafter, regularly submit the status of Infrastructure projects to the City Accounting Office for proper recognition in the books of accounts of the completed projects and the corresponding depreciation.
We also recommended that the City Mayor require the City General Services Officer to immediately file for the insurance coverage of completed projects totaling P8,713,224.40 as consistent with the provisions of Sections 2 and 5 of RA No. 656 and Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992 to ensure indemnity of the value of the said property in case of loss due to fire, earthquake, storm or other casualty.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant made the necessary adjustments for the completed projects. The City Engineering Office conducted the inspection of the infrastructure projects and submitted the status of the 13 infrastructure projects undertaken in CYs 2017 to 2020. The CGSO submitted the copy of the insurance policy of the buildings and other structures.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the City Mayor:
1) require the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to: accomplish the required Inventory and Inspection Report of Property with their technical description for the evaluation of the unserviceable property; complete the computation of salvage value and complete the necessary documents in order to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable property; submit to the COA Audit Team the IIRUP together with their supporting documents for review and evaluation; cause the immediate disposal of the unserviceable property in order to maximize any realizable value from their disposal by sale and the derecognition of the same; and ensure that the disposal of property either thru public bidding or negotiated sale is properly documented and duly authorized.
2) require the City Accountant and the CGSO to finish the reconciliation of their records/reports and provide the same to the Disposal Committee; and
3) require the Accounting Office to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the derecognition of the unserviceable property for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Required Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) with their technical description for the evaluation of the unserviceable property had been accomplished by the Disposal Committee. The IIRUP was submitted on December 29, 2021. The process of disposal of properties thru public bidding and notice of award of sale for the winning bidder was made on December 22, 2021. Partial disposal of properties was undertaken in January 2022 as evidenced by Official Receipts Nos. 5850181-I and 5859347-I dated January 3, 2022 and January 31, 2022, respectively. The City Accountant and the CGSO started reconciliation of their records/reports. The City made adjustments for disposed properties on January 31, 2022.
observations.reason: Various unserviceable PPEs remained undisposed with gross amount of P12,068,907.74 not yet disposed. The IIRUP presented PPEs with gross amount of P16,308,883.97. Disposal was partially effected in January 2022 only. Adjusting entries for disposed properties were made on January 31, 2022.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to:
• seek the assistance of the Appraisal Committee to assess/determine the fair value of the parcel of land which is the subject of lease agreement; • make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts once the valuation has been completed; • submit to the Audit Team the Journal Entry Voucher/s for adjustments made; and • disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the description of the investment property, explanation on why the fair value cannot be determined reliably and range of estimate within which fair value is highly likely to lie, for the fair presentation of the account in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Appraisal Committee determined the fair value of the parcel of land amounting to P26,100,000.00. The City Accountant made adjustments in the books of accounts in April 2021 and submitted JEV No. 101-2021-4-139. The City Accountant disclosed the investment property in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to:
• submit the detailed report of Due from Officers and Employees with validation of its existence and disclose the same in the Notes to Financial Statements; and
• draw JEV to adjust the negative balances in the SLs totaling P19,610.07 for the fair presentation of the account in the financial statements and submit the same to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant submitted the detailed report of the Due from Officers and Employees with tax deficiencies amounting to P116,020.41 and made adjustments for the negative balance in the Subsidiary Ledgers by drawing JEV.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to revert the Accounts Payable of P9,408,621.43 outstanding for two years or more to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Panlungsod with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claims arise in the future, pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445 and in accordance with the requirement of Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant reverted the Accounts Payable of P9,408,621.43 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to:
• submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents as presented in Annex 14 for evaluation; otherwise, the same may be suspended or disallowed in audit; and
• ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Notice of Suspension No. 22-001-101-(20) dated February 14, 2022 was issued for disbursement with net amount of P1,180,711.28. Notice of Disallowance No. 22-001-101-(20) dated February 14, 2022 was issued for disallowed amount of P27,919.64. The City Accountant ensured that all claims against the government funds presented were properly documented.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were submitted and evaluated by COA; certain amounts were suspended/disallowed as noted; other claims were properly documented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Social Welfare and Development Officer (DSWD) and the City Accountant to submit the lacking documents for validation in compliance with DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09 s. 2020 and Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445. Also, ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The City submitted the required supporting documents which were evaluated by the Audit Team and found complete and in order.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Social Welfare and Development Officer and the City Accountant to submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team in compliance with DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09 s. 2020 and Section 4(6) of PD 1445 for validation.
observations.managementAction: The City Social Welfare and Development Officer and the City Accountant submitted the lacking documents to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
• require the ICO-City Engineering Office and the City Architect Office to submit their written explanation for the non-submission of the required documents; • instruct the City General Services Officer to coordinate with the ICO-City Engineering Office and the City Architect for the completion of the required documents and immediately file for the insurance coverage of all the uninsured properties consistent with the provisions of Sections 2 and 5 of RA No. 656 and Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992 to ensure indemnity of the value of the said property in case of loss due to fire, earthquake, storm or other casualty; and • require the City Accountant to come up with the updated list of insurable buildings and other structures with their correct total amount together with the written explanation on why the Updated List of Insurable Buildings and Other Structures as of December 31, 2020 was not provided to the CGSO and to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-City Engineer and the City Architect submitted the required explanation/documents and the CGSO submitted the copy of the insurance policy of the buildings and other structures. The City Accountant provided the CGSO with the updated list of insurable buildings and other structures.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the City Mayor instruct the:
• Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the City Engineer to properly plan and make a schedule/timeline of all procurement activities pertaining to development projects for their timely implementation and completion; • City Planning and Development Officer (CPDO) to strategize and coordinate with all concerned Departments to improve the planning and programming of projects. Also, to fast track and prioritize the full implementation of the development projects as embodied in the 20 percent Development Fund Plan within the year these were programmed in pursuance of the law and ensure that allocated funds are timely and optimally utilized for projects identified in accordance with existing guidelines, and regularly monitor and evaluate the projects funded under the 20 percent DF in order to minimize the number of projects not implemented; and • City Engineer to develop other procedures and method in order to facilitate the preparation of plans and technical design of all projects to be implemented.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee and the City Engineer will properly plan and make a schedule/timeline of all procurement activities pertaining to the development projects for their timely implementation and completion. The City Planning and Development Office committed to strategize and coordinate with all concerned departments to improve the planning and programming of projects and will fast track and prioritize the full implementation of the development projects as embodied in the 20 percent DF planned during the year. The City Engineer committed to develop other procedures and method in order to facilitate the preparation of plans and technical design of all projects to be implemented.
observations.reason: Some developmental projects programmed during CY 2020 were implemented; however, many projects programmed during the year and those under continuing appropriations remained unimplemented or not fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the 35 disbursement vouchers together with their supporting documents for review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The 35 disbursement vouchers were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City General Services Officer (CGSO) to complete the conduct of physical count of all supplies and materials inventory of the City every semester and to prepare and render a complete Report of the Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI) and submit to the COA Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for first and second semesters, respectively; City GSO to prepare Monthly Report of Supplies and Materials for all types of inventory and to submit the same monthly to the Accounting Office as their basis of recording the issuances and to the Audit Team for verification; City Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) for each commodity/stock to facilitate the reconciliation and for verification of the year-end balances; and CGSO and the City Accountant to coordinate for the reconciliation of their records regularly and make the necessary adjustments in their respective records, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Management instructed the CGSO and the City Accountant to relentlessly pursue and sustain effort in the conduct of periodic inventory of all supplies and materials and to coordinate with the City Accountant for the reconciliation of their records, especially the Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) which the latter is required to maintain.
observations.reason: No actual physical count of inventories conducted during the year. No RPCI was submitted to COA Audit Team on July 31, 2020 and January 31, 2021. The Accounting Office continued their practice of maintaining SLCs per account title instead of per commodity/stock. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew in audit observation no. 3, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
• require the City General Services Officer (CGSO) to request from the
Accounting Office the details and descriptions of the adjustments totaling
P9,311,820.97;
• require the City Accountant to confer with the CGSO and to verify the
quantity and amounts of the PPE recorded in lump sum amounts totaling
P5,373,841.56.

Also, we reiterated our recommendation that the CGSO and the City Accountant to reconcile their records, submit to the Audit Team the proof of reconciliation and any adjustments, if warranted, should be made in order that the existence and valuation of the PPE could be
ascertained/established for the fair presentation of the financial statements in
accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City General Services Officer
to:
• continue and finish the conduct of survey of those hereditary properties
which has been started by the previous administration and to seek the
approval of the Land Management Services of the DENR for the titling of
the land;
• coordinate with the heirs of the deceased owner and follow-up the
necessary documents initially requested for three parcels of land; and
• follow up and obtain from the seller the Copy of Certificate of Full Payment
from the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) to facilitate the transfer and
titling of the land to prove ownership and to avoid possible third-party
claims and incurrence of litigation costs in the future in pursuant to
Section 39(2) of PD No.1445 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386
and for the fair presentation of the financial statements in accordance with
Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
• identify those properties with inheritance issues so that same are referred
to concerned heirs for their immediate action and resolution to facilitate
the titling of the 26 parcels of land in the name of the City.

We reiterated our recommendations that the City Mayor require the:
a. City General Services Officer (CGSO) to complete the conduct of physical
count of all supplies and materials inventory of the City every semester
and to prepare and render a complete Report of the Physical Count of
Inventory (RPCI) and submit to the COA Audit Team not later than July 31
and January 31 of each year for first and second semesters, respectively;
b. City GSO to prepare Monthly Report of Supplies and Materials for all types
of inventory and to submit the same monthly to the Accounting Office as
their basis of recording the issuances and to the Audit Team for
verification;
c. City Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) for each
commodity/stock to facilitate the reconciliation and for verification of the
year-end balances; and
d. CGSO and the City Accountant to coordinate for the reconciliation of their
records regularly and make the necessary adjustments in their respective
records, if warranted.

Also, the Management to have a strong internal control on the inventory
management system and ensure that all supplies and materials inventory of
the City are safeguarded and duly accounted for.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
a. City Accountant to:
• coordinate with the City Engineering Office regarding projects
undertaken/completed on a phase-by-phase basis and make the
necessary adjustments to reclassify the 21 completed infrastructure
projects worth P19,474,192.95 to their respective PPE accounts; and
• request from the City Engineering Office the status of the 13
infrastructure projects undertaken in CYs 2017 to 2020 with a total
cost of P6,812,109.21 and one project for upgrading of electrical
facility of health center undertaken in CY 2010 with contract amount
of P109,191.65 which are still recorded in the CIP account and
prepare the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, if
warranted.
b. ICO-City Engineering Office to:
• review and inspect the infrastructure projects implemented by their
Office and submit to the Audit Team and the Accounting Office the
Updated Status of Implementation of Infrastructure Projects
undertaken in CYs 2017-2020. Thereafter, regularly submit the
status of Infrastructure projects to the City Accounting Office for
proper recognition in the books of accounts the completed projects
and the corresponding depreciation.

We also recommended that the City Mayor require the City General Services
Officer to immediately file for the insurance coverage of completed projects
totaling P8,713,224.40 as presented in Annex 10 consistent with the provisions
of Sections 2 and 5 of RA No. 656 and Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 92-390
dated November 17, 1992 to ensure indemnity of the value of the said property
in case of loss due to fire, earthquake, storm or other casualty.

We reiterated our recommendations that the City Mayor
1) require the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to:
• accomplish the required Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) with their technical description for the
evaluation of unserviceable property;
• complete the computation of salvage value and complete the necessary
documents in order to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable property;
• submit to the COA Audit Team the IIRUP together with its supporting
documents for review and evaluation;
• cause the immediate disposal of the unserviceable property in order to
maximize any realizable value from their disposal by sale and the
derecognition of the same; and
• ensure that the disposal of property either thru public bidding or
negotiated sale is properly documented and duly authorized.
2) require the City Accountant and the CGSO to finish the reconciliation of
their records/reports and provide the same to the Disposal Committee; and
3) require the Accounting Office to prepare the necessary adjusting entries
for the derecognition of the unserviceable property for the fair presentation
of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to:
• seek the assistance of the Appraisal Committee to assess/determine the fair value
of the parcel of land which is the subject of lease agreement;
• make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts once the valuation has
been completed;
• submit to the Audit Team the Journal Entry Voucher/s for the adjustments made; and
• disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the description of the
investment property, explanation on why the fair value cannot be
determined reliably and range of estimate within which fair value is highly
likely to lie, for the fair presentation of the account in the financial
statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to:
• submit the detailed report of Due from Officers and Employees with tax
deficiencies amounting to P116,020.41 for validation of its existence and
disclose the same in the Notes to Financial Statements; and
• draw JEV to adjust the negative balances in the SLs totaling P19,610.07,
for the fair presentation of the account in the financial statements in
accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS and submit the same to the Audit
Team.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to revert the
Accounts Payable of P9,408,621.43 outstanding for two years or more to the
Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund of the City subject to an authority
from the Sangguniang Panlungsod with a condition that appropriation shall be
made available in the event that claims arises in the future, pursuant to the
provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445 and in accordance with the
requirement of Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to:
• submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents as
presented in Annex 14 for evaluation;
• submit written explanation on the noted deficiencies. Otherwise, the same may be
suspended or disallowed in audit; and
• ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with
complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) to ensure that the prospective contractor/winning bidder receive the
Notice to Proceed within 7 days upon approval/issuance of the contract since
upon contract signing there should be an approved CSHP from DOLE as
provided in Section 37.2.3(f) of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and
Regulations of Republic Act 9184, in accordance with Section 37.4.1 of the
2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to avoid the delays in the implementation of
the projects of the City.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to submit to
the Audit Team all the required supporting documents for evaluation.
Otherwise, the same may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

Also, ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported
with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Social Welfare and
Development Officer and the City Accountant to submit the lacking documents
to the Audit Team in compliance with DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09 s.
2020 and Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree (PD) 1445 for validation.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City General Services Officer (CGSO) and the City Accountant to continue exerting effort and to be mindful to finish the reconciliation of their records and any adjustments, if warranted, should be made in order that the existence and valuation of the PPE could be ascertained/established for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The CGSO and the Accounting Office continuously reconciling their records.
observations.reason: There are PPE accounts which remain unreconciled and there are PPE items recorded in lump-sum amount. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew in audit observation no. 1, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City General Services Officer (CGSO) to continue and finish the conduct of survey of those hereditary properties which has been started by the previous administration and to seek the approval of the Land Management Services of the DENR for the titling of the land; coordinate with the heirs of the deceased owner and follow-up the necessary documents initially requested for three parcels of land; and follow up and obtain from the seller the Copy of Certificate of Full Payment from the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) to facilitate the transfer and titling of the land to prove ownership and to avoid possible third-party claims and incurrence of litigation costs in the future in pursuant to Section 39(2) of PD No.1445 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and for the fair presentation of the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1. Identify those properties with inheritance issues so that same are referred to concerned heirs for their immediate action and resolution to facilitate the titling of the 26 parcels of land in the name of the City.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to require the CGSO to continually exert extra effort to process the respective TCT of those hereditary properties with inheritance issues.
observations.reason: The 26 parcels of land still remain untitled in the name of the City. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew in audit observation no. 2, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to coordinate with the City Engineering Office regarding projects undertaken/completed on a phase-by-phase basis and make the necessary adjustments to reclassify the completed infrastructure projects to their respective PPE accounts; request from the City Engineering Office the status of the infrastructure projects undertaken in CYs 2013 to 2016 which are still recorded in the CIP account and prepare the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, if warranted; require the ICO-City Engineering Office to review and inspect the infrastructure projects implemented by their Office and submit to the Audit Team and the Accounting Office the Updated Status of Implementation of Infrastructure Projects undertaken in CYs 2013-2016; and require the City General Services Officer to immediately file for the insurance coverage of completed projects consistent with the provisions of Sections 2 and 5 of RA No. 656 and Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 92-390.
observations.managementAction: Further, the Management committed to instruct the City Accountant and the ICO-City Engineer to follow the COA’s recommendations.
observations.reason: There are still completed infrastructure projects which were not yet reclassified to their respective PPE accounts because they were undertaken/completed on a phase-by-phase basis. There are still infrastructure projects undertaken in CY 2010 included in the CYs 2013 to 2016 in the amount of P109,191.65 which were not yet reclassified to their respective PPE accounts. No action has been undertaken to file for insurance of uninsurable properties. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew in audit observation no. 4, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the City Mayor require the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to: accomplish the required Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) with their technical description for the evaluation of unserviceable property; complete the computation of salvage value and complete the necessary documents in order to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable property; submit to the COA Audit Team the IIRUP together with its supporting documents for review and evaluation; cause the immediate disposal of the unserviceable property in order to maximize any realizable value from their disposal by sale and the derecognition of the same; and ensure that the disposal of property either thru public bidding or negotiated sale is properly documented and duly authorized; require the City Accountant and the CGSO to finish the reconciliation of their records/reports and provide the same to the Disposal Committee; and require the Accounting Office to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the derecognition of the unserviceable property for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to require the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to come up with the IIRUP in accordance with the requirements for submission to the COA Audit Team, cause the immediate disposal of the unserviceable property thru public bidding or negotiated sale and require the Accounting Office to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the derecognition of the unserviceable property for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: IIRUP was not implemented/submitted to the Audit Team. The committee has initiated the conduct of inventory and prepared the IIRUP. The committee is still determining the salvage value of those unserviceable properties. No disposal has been conducted during CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to revert to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF those Accounts Payable outstanding for two years or more if no replies were received from creditors after the lapse of the 20-day period, and if no claims have been filed and the creditors failed to support their claims, revert the same to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund. Ensure that the reversion of outstanding Accounts Payable to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF will be subjected to an authority from the Sangguniang Panlungsod with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future, pursuant to the applicable provisions of PD No. 1445 and in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Management instructed the City Accountant to unabatingly follow up from the creditors their replies to the confirmation letters and if there will be little progress on said effort, upon seeking an appropriate Sangguniang Panlungsod’s authorization, revert the balance of Accounts Payable to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, pursuant to the applicable provisions of PD No. 1445 and IPSAS.
observations.reason: Accounts Payable outstanding for more than two years were not reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF; limited progress due to non-responsive creditors. The City Accountant paid four creditors but did not revert all outstanding APs. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew in audit observation no. 8, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the required supporting documents for evaluation. Otherwise, the same may be suspended in audit. Also, ensure that all future disbursements pertaining to honorarium, additional allowances and other benefits to judges, prosecutors, and other national government officials stationed in or assigned to the City are properly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant submitted to the Audit Team the required supporting documents. The Management required the City Accountant to see to it that additional disbursements and allowances to judges, prosecutors and national government officials stationed in or assigned to the City were properly supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Planning and Development Coordinator and the City Engineer to: submit to the Audit Team Leader at the beginning of the year, the complete list of all ongoing government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year with relevant information as required in Item 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004; inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award of the project or before the start of the project that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already posted for prompt validation and inspection of the Audit Team; and regularly submit the monthly monitoring report with supporting documents in compliance with the existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Management required the City Planning and Development Coordinator and the City Engineer to submit to the Audit Team the complete list of all ongoing government PPAs and those to be implemented during the year. The CPDO and the City Engineer regularly submit the monthly monitoring reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City General Services Officer to identify the documents and facilitate the immediate filing for the insurance coverage of all the uninsured properties consistent with the provisions of Sections 2 and 5 of RA No. 656 and Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 92-390; and the City Accountant to come up with the updated list of insurable buildings and other structures with their correct total amount.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to instruct the CGSO to coordinate with ICO-City Engineer and the City Architect for the completion of the documents and immediately file for the insurance coverage of all the uninsured properties. The Management also committed to instruct the City Accountant to come up with the updated list of insurable buildings and other structures with their correct total amount.
observations.reason: Some documents required in the application for insurance of properties were still not completed by the CGSO; application has not yet been filed. The updated list of insurable buildings and other structures with their correct total amount were not yet provided by the City Accountant to the CGSO and the Audit Team. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew in audit observation no. 13, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the City Engineering to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects for their timely implementation and completion; the City Planning and Development Officer (CPDO) to fast track and prioritize the full implementation of the development projects as embodied in the 20% Development Fund Plan during the year these were programmed and ensure that allocated funds are timely and optimally utilized; the City Engineer to develop other procedures and methods to facilitate the preparation of technical design of all projects to be implemented; and continually work for the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund through the implementation of the projects that will contribute to the development of the City.
observations.managementAction: The Management instructed the BAC, the City Engineer and the City Planning and Development Coordinator to continually work for the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund. The effort of the City Administrator to closely coordinate among the offices concerned the orderly and expeditious manner of implementing the various infrastructure projects was sustained.
observations.reason: There are still development projects programmed during CYs 2019 and 2020 that were not fully implemented during CY 2020. Some development projects are still subject to preparation of plans and designs and other procedural steps such as acquisition of Right-of-Way. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew in audit observation no. 14, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to follow up from the creditors their replies to the confirmation letters, and if no replies are received within a reasonable period, revert the outstanding Accounts Payable to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund subject to Sangguniang Panlungsod authority and condition that appropriation be made available in case claim arises in the future.
observations.managementAction: The City Mayor issued a Memorandum directing the City Accountant to follow up on replies to the confirmation letters and to revert the outstanding balance of Accounts Payable to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund after compliance with requirements.
observations.reason: There has been very little progress due to non-responsive creditors; confirmations not received; reversion to Unappropriated Surplus pending compliance with PD No. 1445 requirements and Sangguniang Panlungsod authorization.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the CGSO to exert extra effort in obtaining the required documents for processing of the land title in the name of the City and require the City Administrator to turn over to the CGSO the documents for the 23 parcels of land for immediate titling.
observations.managementAction: The City Mayor issued an Office Memorandum directing the CGSO to obtain required documents for processing of TCTs and instructed the City Administrator to turn-over documents for the 23 parcels of land to facilitate titling.
observations.reason: The remaining untitled parcels are hereditary/old properties left by the previous administration; survey and required documents remain incomplete and not yet approved by DENR Land Management Services; documents for the 23 lots were still not turned over as of report date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City General Services Officer to identify the documents and facilitate their completion by coordinating with the GSIS Lucena City Office for immediate filing for insurance coverage of the uninsured property, and the City Accountant to come up with the updated list of insurable buildings and other structures with their correct total amount.
observations.managementAction: The City Mayor issued a Memorandum instructing the City Accountant to come up with the updated list of insurable buildings and other structures and instructing the CGSO to file for insurance coverage of the uninsured properties with GSIS.
observations.reason: Application for insurance was submitted to GSIS Lucena City Branch but was not accepted due to absence of some documents; updated list and required documents were not yet provided or completed as of report date.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor require the City General Services Officer
and the City Accountant to continue exerting their effort to complete and finish
the reconciliation of their records and any adjustments, if warranted, should be
made in order that the existence and valuation of the PPE could be
ascertained/established for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the
financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City General Services Officer
(CGSO) to continually exert extra effort in obtaining the required documents
for the processing of the land title in the name of the City.

We also recommended that the City Mayor require the City Administrator to
immediately turn-over to the CGSO the documents for the 23 parcels of land
for the immediate titling so that the interest of the City Government is
protected from possible third party claims and incurrence of litigation costs in
the future pursuant to Section 39(2) of PD No. 1445 and Section 148 of COA
Circular No. 92-386, and in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
a. City General Services Officer (CGSO) to pursue and complete the
conduct of physical count of all supplies and materials inventory of the
City every semester and to prepare and render a complete Report on
Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI) and submit to the COA Audit Team
not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for first and second
semesters, respectively;
b. City Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) for each
commodity/stock and the Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) for each inventory
account for verification of the year-end balances; and
c. CGSO and the City Accountant to coordinate for the reconciliation of
their records regularly and make the necessary adjustments in their
respective records, if warranted.

Also, the Management to adopt a strong internal control on the inventory
management system and ensure that all supplies and materials inventory of
the City are safeguarded and duly accounted for.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
a. City Accountant to:
• make the necessary adjustments to reclassify the 24 completed
infrastructure projects worth P76,201,181.79 to their respective PPE
accounts; and
• require the City Engineering Office to submit a report indicating the
status of the 50 infrastructure projects undertaken in CYs 2013 to
2016 with a total cost of P129,035,822.51 which are still recorded in
the CIP account and prepare the necessary adjustments in the
books of accounts, if warranted.

b. ICO-City Engineering Office to:
• review and inspect the infrastructure projects implemented by their
Office and submit to the Audit Team and provide the Accounting
Office of the Updated Status of Implementation of Infrastructure
Projects undertaken in CYs 2013 to 2016; and
• regularly submit to the City Accounting Office the status of
Infrastructure projects implemented for proper recognition in the
books of accounts of completed projects and corresponding
depreciation.

c. City General Services Officer to immediately file for the insurance
coverage of all the uninsured property consistent with the provisions of
Sections 2 and 5 of RA No. 656 and Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 92-
390 dated November 17, 1992 to ensure indemnity of the value of the
said property in case of loss due to fire, earthquake, storm or other
casualty.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
• create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee that will facilitate the
appraisal, inspection and disposal of the unserviceable property;
• require the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to accomplish the
required Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property
(IIRUP) with their technical description for the evaluation of
unserviceable property;
• submit to the COA Audit Team the IIRUP together with its supporting
documents for review and evaluation;
• cause the immediate disposal of the unserviceable property in order to
maximize any realizable value from their disposal by sale. and the
derecognition of the same;
• require the Accounting Office to prepare the necessary adjusting
entries for the derecognition of the unserviceable property for the fair
presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements; and
• ensure that the disposal of property either thru public bidding or
negotiated sale is properly documented and duly authorized.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor instruct the City
Accountant to send to creditors request for confirmation of the amount of
P512,826.00 to determine if the claims are still valid and existing
prescribing a 20-day period within which to submit their replies and supporting
documents, and to revert to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund
the balance of Accounts Payable totaling P1,220,778.79 which have been
outstanding for more than two years and for which no replies were received
after the lapse of the 20-day period and even for almost a year now no claims
have been filed.

Likewise, as to the outstanding Accounts Payable of P1,839,667.71 obligated in
CY 2017, instruct the City Accountant to require the creditors to immediately
submit the necessary documents pertaining to their claims to the City. In the
event that they fail to support their claims, revert the same to the
unappropriated surplus of the GF.

Ensure that the reversion of outstanding Accounts Payable to the
Unappropriated Surplus of the GF will be subjected to an authority from the
Sangguniang Panlungsod with a condition that appropriation shall be made
available in the event that claim arises in the future, pursuant to the provisions
of Section 98 of PD No. 1445 and with the requirement of Paragraph 27 of
IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to submit to
the Audit Team all the required supporting documents for evaluation.
Otherwise, the same may be suspended in audit.

Also, ensure that all future disbursements pertaining to honorarium, additional
allowances and other benefits to judges, prosecutors, and other national
government officials stationed in or assigned to the City were properly
supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4(6) of
PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Planning and
Development Coordinator and the City Engineer to:
• submit to the Audit Team Leader at the beginning of the year, the
complete list of all ongoing government projects/programs/activities
and those that are to be implemented during the year with relevant
information as required in Item 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004;
• inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award of the
infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the
appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already
posted for prompt validation and inspection of the Audit Team; and
• regularly submit the monthly monitoring report with supporting
documents in compliance with the existing regulations.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the CGSO and the City Accountant to complete and finish the reconciliation of their records and any adjustments, if warranted, should be made in order that the valuation and existence of the PPE could be ascertained/ established for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The City Mayor issued a Memorandum instructing the CGSO and the City Accountant to complete and finish the reconciliation of their records.
observations.reason: There are PPE accounts which remain unreconciled. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew in audit observation no. 1, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the: a. CGSO to conduct the physical count of all inventories of the City and to prepare and submit inventories and required RPCI; b. City Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) for each commodity/stock and the Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) for each inventory account; and c. CGSO and the City Accountant to reconcile their records regularly and make the necessary adjustments in their respective records, if warranted. We also recommended that provision for storage room/stock room be considered in the construction of the New City Hall.
observations.managementAction: The City Mayor issued a Memorandum requiring the CGSO and the City Accountant to prepare and submit the required RPCI. The Design and Build Committee (DBC) informed the City Mayor that the provision on storage/stockroom has been considered in the Site Development and Construction of the New City Hall and was included in the Terms of Reference of the project. The City Accountant maintained SLCs (categorized per account title) and stated SLs will be submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The CGSO was not able to conduct actual physical count of supplies and materials inventory as of December 31, 2019; RPCI was not prepared and submitted. SLCs were maintained by the City Accountant but categorized per account title and not per commodity/stock; SLs were not submitted as of report date. Lack of storage facility resulted in inventories being immediately issued to end users upon receipt.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant made the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee; require the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to accomplish the required Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) with their technical description for the evaluation of unserviceable property; submit to the COA Audit Team the IIRUP together with its supporting documents for review and evaluation; cause the immediate disposal of the unserviceable property; require the Accounting Office to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the derecognition of the unserviceable property; and ensure that the disposal of property either thru public bidding or negotiated sale is properly documented and duly authorized.
observations.managementAction: The Management created a City Disposal Committee which initiated the conduct of inventory of various unserviceable property of the City and has started collating necessary documents for appraisal and disposal. The Committee has yet to determine salvage values thru the City Appraisal Committee for eventual disposal.
observations.reason: The Appraisal and Disposal Committee has not completed the determination of salvage values and the required supporting documents remain incomplete; monitoring of disposal processes was lacking, delaying disposal and derecognition.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct all service vehicle custodians for timely submission of requirements to the City General Services Office to renew registration of all serviceable motor vehicles within prescribed period with the LTO to avoid penalties.
observations.managementAction: The City Mayor issued a circular to all service vehicle custodians for timely submission of requirements to the City General Services Office and to renew registrations within prescribed periods.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor continually work for the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund through implementation of projects that will contribute to the development of the City; require coordination among City Administrator, City Architect, City Engineering Office, City Planning and Development Coordinator and the Bids and Awards Committee; and adopt Project Implementation Management Plans with maximum timelines.
observations.managementAction: The City Administrator issued a directive to the LGU’s Infrastructure Cluster (City Planning and Development Coordinator, City Engineering Office and City Architect) to adopt Project Implementation Management Plans and coordinate implementation of infrastructure projects; the City Administrator has been coordinating with concerned offices resulting in improvements in 20% DF utilization.
observations.reason: There remain substantial unutilized portions largely due to big-ticket projects requiring long implementation periods (e.g., East Road and West Road) and procedural steps such as acquisition of right-of-way; time lag from prior-year allocations continues to affect full utilization.

observations.recommendation: « require the CGSO and the City Accountant to complete and finish the reconciliation of their records and any adjustments, if warranted, should be made in order that the valuation and existence of the PPE could be ascertained/established for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the Financial Statements;

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City General
Services Officer to coordinate with the City Accountant for the proper and
periodic reconciliation of records of the PPE and any adjustments should be
made in order to present the accurate balance of the PPE accounts in the
Financial Statements pursuant to Section 114 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume L

Management's Comment:

The City Accountant commented that the City General Services Office
conducted physical inventory in November 2017, Any purchases will be added to
the report, however they failed to reconcile their records at year-end.

The City General Services Officer informed the Audit Team that a physical
inventory will be conducted again in June 2018. They committed to reconcile their
records with the Accounting Office until August 2018.

Auditor's Rejoinder:

The Audit Team acknowledges the comment of the City Accountant that
the CGSO conducted physical inventory in Nov 2017. However as per validation
of the Audit Team as indicated on the table shown above, the City Accountant and
the CGSO failed to reconcile their records as of year-end.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:

a. City General Services Officer to:
* conduct the physical count of all inventories of the City;
* prepare and submit immediately the required RPCI; and

* maintain complete stock cards

b. CGSO and the City Accountant to reconcile their records
regularly; and

¢. City Accountant to prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger
Cards and to take up the necessary entries to adjust the affected
accounts.

Management's Comment,

The CGSO explained that the City Treasurer’s Office and the City Health
Office have separate control on the issuance of their supplies. Monthly reports on
issuances were submitted to the Accounting Office which serve as basis in
recording the Journal Entry Vouchers for the cost of used supplies.

The CGSO further committed to perform physical inventory on supplies in
July 2018 and to reconcile records with the Accounting Office.

Auditor's Rejoinder:

The comment of the CGSO is noted, however the Audit Team reiterates
that the concerned officers follow the recommendations stated above.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to
confirm with account owners/creditors and to request them to submit
documents pertaining to their claims to the City Government. In the event
that they fail to support their claims, revert the account to the unappropriated
surplus of the General Fund pursuant to Section 98 of PD 1445, subject to an
authority from the Sangguniang Panlungsod with a condition that
appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the
future,

Management's Comment

The City Accountant committed to confirm with the concerned creditors if
there are still any existing obligations due them.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor instruct the:

« City Accountant to:

a. reconcile her records on the balances of Buildings and Other
Structures in order to facilitate the transfer of structures to the
concerned barangays. Otherwise, the insurance of said properties will
be assumed by the City; and

b. submit the updated list of insurable Buildings and Other Structures
with their correct total cost to the Audit Team;

¢ In Charge of Office (ICO) — City Engineer to prepare, issue and
furnish Certificate of Completion to the Property Officer who will
collate the necessary documents for property insurance; and

* City General Services Officer to file insurance for the uninsured
properties with the GSIS consistent with the provisions of Sections
2 and § of RA No. 656 and Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 92-390
dated November 17, 1992.

Management's Comment:

The City General Services Officer stated that the Certificate of Occupancy
which is necessary for property insurance was not issued to the constructed
Buildings and Other Structures due to the pending charges payable to the Bureau
of Fire and Protection (BFP).

The ICO - City Engineer clarified that some of these charges were already
paid by the City resulting in the issuance of the corresponding Certificate of
Occupancy. He committed to submit the revised list of Building and Other
Structures with Certificate of Occupancy.

As regards the Buildings and Other Structures being used by the barangays,
the City Accountant explained that the process of donating these structures to the
concerned barangay is already on-going and a Memorandum of Agreement will be
executed between the City and the said barangay.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor require the
City General Services Officer to provide the status on the titling of the parcels
of land to the Audit Team and to exert best effort in facilitating the titling of
the same.

%
Management's Comment:

The City General Services Officer informed the Audit Team that the land
titles were turned-over by the City Treasurer to the City Administrator but is yet to
be transferred to her. Further, some of the acquired lots in CY 2017 were already
transferred in the name of the City. However, the seven parcels of land with
assessed value of £3,028,570.00 remained untitled.

The City Accountant also informed that the City of Tayabas hired a
Geodetic Engineer who will conduct the survey of the parcels of land acquired in
the prior years but he resigned from Office.

Auditor's Rejoinder:

It is further recommended that the City Mayor require the City
Administrator to turn-over to the City General Services Officer the titles which
were turned-over by the City Treasurer.

We reiterated our recommendation for the full utilization of the
20% Development Fund through the implementation of the projects that will
contribute to the development of the City which will be beneficial to its
constituents.

Management's Comment:

The ICO - City Engineer stated that the projects in CY 2015 under the
Continuing Appropriation were already completed as of April 2018. He committed
to fully implement the projects under the 20% Development Fund and to submit to
the Audit Team an updated list of completed infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the LDRRM Officer to
properly identify and categorize programs, projects and activities that are to
be properly charged to LDRRMF and to utilize the fund strictly for calamity
related activities and stop the utilization of LDRRMF for activities other than
those specified under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum
Circular.

Management's Comment:

The LDRRM Officer explained that he just assumed his post in CY 2017.
He then followed the 2016 Local Disaster Risk and Reduction Plan prepared by the
previous LDRRM Officer, however expenditures were not charged in CY 2016
due to the late approval of the Annual Budget.

47
He further committed to stop the utilization of LDRRMF for activities that
should not be charged against the fund and to properly identify and categorize
programs, projects and activities pursuant to the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the BAC Secretariat
and concerned employees to fumish the Auditor with copies of the contracts
and its supporting documents within five working days from perfection
thereof, for timely auditorial review, technical evaluation and inspection.

Management's Comment:

The BAC Secretariat committed to furnish the Auditor with the copies of
the contracts and its supporting documents within five working days from
perfection thereof.

We recommended that the persons liable for the payment of penalties
refund the total amount of £16,670.00 for failure to register the vehicles on
time. Henceforth, require the City General Services Officer to renew the
registration of all serviceable motor vehicles within the prescribed period with
the LTO to avoid payment of penalties.

Management's Comment:
The CGSO informed that she just recently assumed her position and noted
that these penalties were incurred due to the non-monitoring of the registration of
motor vehicles by the previous CGSO.

49
The Executive Assistant committed that the City Mayor will issue a
Memorandum ordering the person/s liable to pay the penalties on the late
registration of vehicles and a copy will be furnished to the Audit Team.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor require the
General Services Officer to effect the immediate disposal of the unserviceable
properties in order to maximize any realizable value from their disposal by
sale.

Management's Comment:

The General Services Officer informed that a Disposal Committee was
created by the City Mayor, however the members do not want to perform their
duties until they have undergone formal training of Property Disposal. She further
stated that the City Mayor have formally requested the COA Regional Office No.
IV-A 1o conduct the said training.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

 require the City Assessor and the City GSO to exert effort and take the
necessary steps in obtaining the pertinent documents for the titling of the 14
parcels of land in the name of the City;

o instruct the Appraisal Committee to complete the appraisal of the two lots
to ensure fair presentation of the Land account in the financial statements;
and

 require the City Accountant to record the said lots using their fair value as
of the date of acquisition in accordance with Paragraph 27 of PPSAS 17
based on available evidence of ownership.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

o instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) to submit immediately to the
Audit Team for audit verification purposes, the list of insurable Buildings
and Structures with their correct total cost which includes the costs of
improvement;

o instruct the City Engineer to prepare, issue and furnish Certificate of
Completion to the Property Officer who will collate the necessary
documents for property insurance; and

o instruct the City GSO to insure the uninsured properties with the GSIS,
consistent with the provisions of Sections 1 and 5 of RA No. 656 and
Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November 17, 1992.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City General
Services Officer to facilitate the immediate registration of the unregistered
vehicles with the LTO in compliance with Section 5 of RA No. 4136.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City General
Services Officer (GSO) to immediately file the insurance for the uninsured six
motor vehicles with the GSIS, consistent with the provisions of Sections 2 and
5 of RA No. 656 and Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November
17, 1992.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:

e Disbursing Officer to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 5.8 of
COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 by immediately
refunding the unexpended balance; and

* City Accountant to receive the Liquidation Report for cash advances for
seminars, trainings and travel expenses with unexpended balances, only if
it is supported with the Official Receipt (OR) representing payment for the
refund of the unexpended balances.

We recommended that the amount of P68,464.28 representing
disbursements not related to the intended purpose of the SEF be refunded.

Henceforth, the Local School Board strictly observe the provisions of
Section 1 of RA No. 5447, Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and other rules and
regulations, relating to the defined usage or utilization of the SEF.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City GSO to
consider procuring common-use supplies from the PS-DBM or its depots in
accordance with the provisions of AO No. 17, s. 2011, to save on cost, people
involvement and other resources.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer to
strictly comply with the requirements stated under COA Circular No. 92-389
to prevent the possible re-use of those documents for other claims of the same
nature as part of a sound internal control system in the disbursement of
funds.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Treasurer to
account for the P9,331,553.88 representing contributions of the 66 barangays,
otherwise refund the share of each barangay from the Special Health Fund of
the same amount.

Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of Article 61(d) of
Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Local Government Code of 1991
for any future similar cooperative undertakings.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer and
the City Accountant to remit the RPT share due to the component Barangays
within the preseribed period, in accordance with Section 271(d) of RA No.
7160,

We recommended that the City Mayor:

* see to it that the City is provided with the list of individuals/entity that
will undertake selling activity/ies before issuing the permit for the
temporary use of street, sidewalk, or alley or portion of the City;

e instruct the City Treasurer to collect the required fee of P150.00 per day
per individual/entity; and

* assign checkers, if warranted, to validate the number of individuals/entity
that will undertake selling activity/ies, so that the amounts supposed to be
collected will be collected.

We recommended the full utilization of the 20% Development Fund
through the implementation of the projects that will contribute to the
development of the City which will be beneficial to its constituents.
